text: pool.ts: pool-on 원격 exec에 setsid+timeout+cleanup trap 배선 — ssh 세션 드롭 시 원격 커맨드 트리 자동 수거(고아 fork storm 근절 · convergence pool-ts-1)

commons.md = 9896B / 10000 cap · headroom 104B. This is the genuine FLOOR — do not retry compression. Evidence: a 32-rule adversarial audit (57 agents, workflow wf_de90201f-563) refuted 12 of 13 proposed compressions as lossy and confirmed 0 drift. The audits headline recommendation J1 ("move pi5-akida-anima + hexa-lang-model to .harness overrides = -647B, the only reduction free of normative loss") is REFUTED on BOTH counts — do not implement it: (1) hexa-lang-model has 12 sidecar-managed consumer repos with .hexa sources (airgenome anima bio demiurge drive forge gamebox hexa-codex hexa-lang phanes pool secret), so an override move means 12 duplicated copies to keep in sync — strictly worse than one shared rule; (2) pi5-akida-anima s dont ("Registering it in the shared pool · repurposing it for build/bench/CI/GPU") targets NON-anima repos — anima is the one repo that would never violate it — so moving it to anima/.harness/commons.md deletes the guard exactly where it is needed. Since #422 the override MERGES per slug, so such a move is now mechanically possible — which is precisely why this refutation must be recorded. Remaining option if a future rule must be added: raise lint.commonsCap (harness.config.json), which the lint message itself sanctions "only if genuinely irreducible" — the irreducibility is now evidenced. That is an owner decision (the cap exists to fight per-turn context-rot). Separately open: 19 repos still carry the fossil scratchDir=scripts/scratch (#52 unified to state/); the self-contradicting inject is fixed (#421) so this is consistency-only, and migrating moves real files across 19 repos = owner call.
